feature: '+' button at the end of the columns row to add a new column/status
last_worker_summary: I rewrote `tests/board/columns.add_column.spec.mjs` with 13 `expect()` calls using a two-vector strategy to catch the saboteur: **Assertion 1** finds the button by its stable CSS class `.jira-col-add` (structural, immune to label changes), **Assertion 2** then checks `toHaveAccessibleName('Create status')` on that element (catches any aria-label or img-alt rename), and **Assertion 3** independently verifies `getByRole('button', { name: 'Create status' })` resolves to exactly one element — so either the class disappears *or* the accessible name changes, the test fails before ever reaching the dialog mutations.
mutation_failure_reason: spec STAYED GREEN after feature was removed — the test is not load-bearing. Tighten its assertions against the specific DOM the feature produces.
